note: Daily scrape for Tuesday 2026-05-05. Two new pr-marketing leads cleared the bar from the 5/4-5/5 window: (1) Reserv — $125M Series C 5/4 led by KKR, NY-based AI-native TPA for P&C insurance, $100M ARR, 200 customers, ambition to scale from 500K to 30M complex claims/year (combined 87 — A-tier, fresh post-funding press cycle); (2) Versana — $43M raise closed 4/30 led by BNP Paribas with Fitch Ventures/MassMutual Ventures/Motive Partners/Apollo, NY-based digital infrastructure for the $9T syndicated loan + private credit markets, $4.1T in active facility coverage, ex-BofA/Morgan Stanley/Bloomberg CEO Cynthia Sachs (combined 83 — A-tier border, 5 days old so window closing). Skipped: Sierra ($950M Series E 5/4 — late-stage Bret Taylor unicorn, established comms function, 40% of Fortune 500 — Aidoc-style skip rule applies); True Anomaly ($600M Series D defense — out of scope); Latus Bio ($97M Series A — biotech, out of scope); Sierra ($950M — too late stage); Critical Loop ($26M 4/13 — 22 days old, missed window); Era ($11M 4/23 — consumer-AI hardware, poor FS/Tech fit); Joyful Health (4/16 — 19 days old, missed window); Performativ + Ineffable (non-US HQs). Fund-formation, legal-consulting, coaching-ops, ai-consulting, legal-freelance, and pr-freelance pipelines surfaced no specific named-and-dated postings within the 72-hour rubric window — searches returned aggregator boards and evergreen listings rather than fresh role-specific posts. Signal refresh: Fun ($72M 5/1) Day 4 of press cycle, no new press visible but agency-pitch window still hot through 5/11; JuliaHub Dyad 3.0 launch confirmed for 5/19 with Synopsys + Mitsubishi Electric Research Labs + Binnies + Boeing as named speakers — agency-pitch sweet spot is NOW (5/4-5/12, 14 days to launch); Rogo customer roster (Rothschild/Jefferies/Lazard/Moelis/Nomura) + 35K users / 250 institutions fully disclosed in press, post-peak but still actionable.

leads.name: Reserv
leads.industry: AI-Native Third-Party Administrator (TPA) and Claims Technology / Property & Casualty Insurance Infrastructure (Insurtech)
leads.fundingStage: Series C ($125M, May 4, 2026) led by KKR via its Next Generation Technology Growth strategy, with participation from existing backers Bain Capital Ventures and Flourish Ventures plus select strategic partners and clients. Total reported equity funding now ~$196M. Founded 2022 by CJ Przybyl and Martha Dreiling.
leads.source: BusinessWire (5/4) + VentureBeat (5/4) + Pulse 2.0 (5/4) + AlleyWatch Daily Funding Report 5/4 + fintech.global (5/4) + Finsmes (5/4) + Yahoo Finance (5/4) + PE Insights Substack (5/4)
leads.notes: Strongest new lead of the week. $125M Series C led by KKR's Next Gen Tech Growth fund — that's the same fund that has historically backed insurance-vertical infrastructure plays like Verisk/Applied Systems acquisition-grade companies. Reserv has reached $100M ARR in just 4 years (founded 2022), with ~200 customers spanning insurers, corporate captives, MGAs, and brokers. The narrative is clean: AI-native TPA replacing the legacy P&C claims administration stack, with a stated ambition to scale processed claims from 500,000/year today to 30 million/year over the next four years. That 60x scaling claim is a textbook plant-the-flag pr-marketing setup — it implies an aggressive hiring cycle, a regulatory/compliance narrative (TPAs are state-regulated entities), and a clear category-creator positioning. Buyer fit is strong: they sell B2B to insurers and MGAs, exactly the kind of regulated FS-adjacent buyer where Mark's translation-edge experience matters. Budget at Series C with KKR-led growth equity is the highest-credibility tier — agency retainers in this segment are typically $25-$50k/mo. Comms-relationship odds: STRONG. KKR-led growth rounds typically come with a press cycle expectation: there's almost always a (a) Series C announcement push (5/4 — DONE), (b) named-customer case study cycle (next 4-8 weeks), (c) hiring milestone announcement (next 6-12 weeks tied to the 30M-claims commitment), (d) regulatory/compliance category-leadership thought-leadership track. Reserv is large enough to have an in-house comms function but the press release lists no Head of Comms quote — only the CEO and the KKR partner — which is a common signal that comms is either junior, fractional, or vendor-managed. Recommend a 90-day pitch threading: (a) the 'AI-native TPA replacing the 500K-to-30M-claims-per-year stack' category-creation narrative, (b) the founder-origin story (CJ Przybyl previously founded Snapsheet — there's a published founder track record in insurtech), (c) the KKR validation as 'institutional capital chooses Reserv' angle for trade press (Insurance Journal, Reactions, Best's Review, Carrier Management), (d) crossover B2B-tech press (TechCrunch, Forbes, WSJ enterprise software). Time-sensitive: Series C press cycles run 7-14 days max, clock started 5/4, so peak window is 5/4-5/14 (we're on day 1). Outreach in the next 48-72 hours is optimal. Lead-touch at CEO Przybyl since he's the named press contact in the 5/4 release. Skip ai-consulting (Reserv is the vendor, not a consulting buyer). Combined score 87 — strongest new pr-marketing lead since Fun (5/1, 88). NY-based, in-network, and category-defining trigger event.

leads.name: Versana
leads.industry: Enterprise Data and Digital Infrastructure for the Broadly Syndicated Loan (BSL) and Private Credit Markets (B2B Fintech / Capital Markets Infrastructure)
leads.fundingStage: $43M capital raise closed April 30, 2026, led by BNP Paribas, with new strategic investments from Fitch Ventures, MassMutual Ventures, Motive Partners and Apollo. Existing shareholders (Bank of America, Barclays, Citi, Deutsche Bank, J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley, U.S. Bancorp, Wells Fargo) all made follow-on investments. Total reported funding above $125M.

contacts.name: Cynthia E. Sachs
contacts.title: Founding CEO (ex-Bank of America, ex-Morgan Stanley, ex-Bloomberg BVAL — leveraged finance + fintech veteran)

leads.source: PR Newswire (4/30) + Morningstar (4/30) + Versana official site (4/30) + Pulse 2.0 (4/30) + Financial IT (4/30) + Finovate (4/30) + Coverager (4/30) + fintech.global (4/30) + SuperbCrew + EQS News
leads.notes: Strong A-tier candidate but window closing — round closed 4/30, today is 5/5 (5 days old, peak press cycle is days 1-7). Worth pitching urgently in the next 48 hours before the news cycle goes cold. Versana is the rare capital-markets infrastructure play with both (a) a strategic-investor-heavy cap table that doubles as customer validation (BNP Paribas leads + Fitch Ventures + MassMutual + Motive + Apollo new + 8 GSIBs follow-on), and (b) a quantifiable proof point that's PR gold ($4.1T in active facility coverage on a platform addressing the $9T BSL + private credit market). The strategic-round structure (vs. a traditional VC growth round) is a comms-favorable narrative because every bank investor is implicitly a customer — that's a very different press hook than 'KKR led the round.' CEO Cynthia Sachs is press-known in fintech/leveraged-finance circles (ex-BofA, ex-Morgan Stanley, ex-Bloomberg BVAL) and her bio alone makes the 'why now / why Versana' angle write itself. The narrative threads available: (a) syndicated loan markets are the largest under-digitized capital markets segment ($9T) and Versana is now the de facto standard, (b) the private-credit explosion (post-Apollo investment) creates a B2B-press hook that crosses Bloomberg/WSJ/FT/Reuters fintech beats, (c) the institutional-strategic-investor cap table (8 GSIBs + BNP Paribas + Apollo + MassMutual) is itself the credibility moat, (d) Sachs as the FS-veteran founder is a women-in-finance press angle that's evergreen in trade press. CAVEATS: (i) round was 4/30 so window is half-spent, (ii) Versana already has total funding >$125M which means there's likely an existing comms/IR function or agency in place — Mark would be pitching against incumbency, (iii) the strategic-investor structure means existing bank-relationships may already be funneling to a specific agency. Recommend: (a) priority outreach in the next 48-72 hours before the window closes, (b) lead-touch at CEO Sachs with a specific narrative pitch (the 'why Versana matters to the $9T market' framing rather than a generic agency overview), (c) consider angle that emphasizes Mark's regulated-FS experience translating well to the BSL + private credit space, (d) skip if first-touch reveals an existing retained agency. Combined score 83 — A-tier border, would be 88+ if discovered on day 1 instead of day 5. NY-based, in-network. Skip legal-consulting/legal-freelance (Versana is software-not-law); skip pr-freelance (no Head of Comms job posting visible).
